results.title: Solutions Architect
results.description:
You see systems, not just servers. While everyone else is zoomed in on one piece of the puzzle, you're the one asking "but how does it all fit together?" — and then drawing the diagram that makes it click for everyone in the room.

Solutions Architects design cloud systems that are reliable, scalable, and cost-effective. You're the bridge between what the business needs and what the technology can do. You don't just build — you design. And then you explain your design so well that everyone from the CTO to the junior dev understands why it works.
day_in_the_life: Morning: Review a client's current infrastructure and sketch out a migration plan to AWS/Azure/GCP
Late morning: Lead a design workshop with the dev team, whiteboarding the architecture for a new microservices platform
Afternoon: Write a Well-Architected Review — assessing security, cost, reliability, and performance
End of day: Present your architecture to senior stakeholders, fielding questions and refining the design

first_cert.name: AWS Cloud Practitioner
first_cert.why: Gives you the cloud vocabulary and foundational understanding every SA needs

second_cert.name: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
second_cert.why: The industry-standard SA credential — this is what hiring managers look for first

first_cert.name: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
first_cert.why: Skip the basics — you already have the fundamentals, go straight to the SA credential

second_cert.name: AWS Solutions Architect Professional
second_cert.why: The gold standard — this cert separates senior SAs from the rest

skills: Cloud architecture patterns (well-architected frameworks)
Diagramming and technical documentation (Lucidchart, draw.io)
Networking fundamentals (VPCs, subnets, DNS, load balancing)
Cost optimisation and FinOps basics
Stakeholder communication and technical storytelling
results.sholas_take: I'm a Solutions Architect at AWS, so I'm biased — but I genuinely think this is one of the most rewarding roles in cloud. You get to think big, solve complex problems, AND talk to people. It's the role where non-tech backgrounds are actually an advantage. My economics degree? Turns out understanding business value is half the job. If you got this result, you've got the right brain for it.
results.share_text: I took the CloudPath Quiz and got Solutions Architect — The Big Picture Thinker! Which cloud career fits you?
results.og_description: Your CloudPath result: Solutions Architect. Design cloud systems, bridge business and technology, and earn £45K-£150K+.

results.title: Cloud/DevOps Engineer
results.description:
You don't want to draw the diagram — you want to build the thing. Cloud and DevOps Engineers are the people who make infrastructure actually work. While the architect designs it, you deploy it, automate it, and make sure it runs at 2am without anyone having to wake up.

You love the terminal. You love automation. You love that satisfying feeling of watching a deployment pipeline run green from commit to production. If "infrastructure as code" sounds exciting rather than boring, this is your path.
day_in_the_life: Morning: Write Terraform modules to provision a new Kubernetes cluster
Mid-morning: Debug a failing CI/CD pipeline — find the issue, fix it, push the fix
Afternoon: Automate a manual deployment process that's been annoying the team for months
Late afternoon: Review a pull request for infrastructure changes, pair with a junior engineer

first_cert.name: AWS Cloud Practitioner
first_cert.why: Builds your cloud foundations before diving into the engineering side

second_cert.name: AWS SysOps Administrator Associate
second_cert.why: Hands-on operational skills — the bread and butter of cloud engineering

first_cert.name: AWS SysOps Administrator Associate
first_cert.why: Your sysadmin experience translates directly — skip the basics and go operational

second_cert.name: AWS DevOps Engineer Professional
second_cert.why: The professional-level DevOps cert — proves you can design and manage CI/CD at scale

skills: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation, or Pulumi)
CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins)
Containers and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes, EKS/ECS)
Linux administration and shell scripting (Bash, Python)
Monitoring and observability (CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana)
results.sholas_take: DevOps Engineers are the most in-demand cloud role in the UK right now. Every company needs someone who can automate their infrastructure. What I love about this path is that you see results fast — you build something, it works (or it doesn't, and you fix it), and there's no ambiguity about whether you're adding value. If you like building things with your hands, this is the cloud equivalent.
results.share_text: I took the CloudPath Quiz and got Cloud/DevOps Engineer — The Builder! Which cloud career fits you?
results.og_description: Your CloudPath result: Cloud/DevOps Engineer. Build infrastructure, automate everything, and earn £35K-£140K+.

results.title: Cloud Security Specialist
results.description:
You're the person who sees risk where others see features. While everyone else is rushing to ship, you're the one asking "but is it secure?" — and you're right to ask, because breaches cost companies millions and careers.

Cloud Security Specialists protect cloud environments from threats, design secure architectures, and make sure companies meet compliance requirements. It's a role where attention to detail isn't just nice to have — it's the entire job. And demand for this skill? Through the roof.
day_in_the_life: Morning: Run a security assessment on a client's AWS account — IAM policies, encryption, network exposure
Mid-morning: Review CloudTrail logs to investigate suspicious activity flagged overnight
Afternoon: Design a zero-trust network architecture for a financial services client
Late afternoon: Write a security findings report and present remediation priorities to the engineering team

first_cert.name: CompTIA Security+
first_cert.why: The foundational security cert — gives you the security vocabulary and concepts before specialising in cloud

second_cert.name: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
second_cert.why: Security needs architectural context first — you need to understand what you're protecting

first_cert.name: AWS Security Specialty
first_cert.why: Your security fundamentals are solid — go straight to the cloud security specialisation

second_cert.name: CCSP (Certified Cloud Security Professional)
second_cert.why: The gold standard for cloud security — vendor-neutral and globally recognised

skills: Identity and Access Management (IAM policies, least privilege, federation)
Encryption and key management (KMS, TLS, at-rest/in-transit)
Compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR, PCI-DSS)
Threat detection and incident response (GuardDuty, Security Hub, SIEM tools)
Network security (firewalls, WAFs, VPC security groups, NACLs)
results.sholas_take: Security specialists are the most recession-proof people in cloud. Every breach that makes the news creates more demand for what you do. I work with security architects at AWS regularly, and what strikes me is that many of them came from non-traditional backgrounds — audit, compliance, risk management, even military. If you're naturally cautious and detail-oriented, those are superpowers in this role, not weaknesses.
results.share_text: I took the CloudPath Quiz and got Cloud Security Specialist — The Guardian! Which cloud career fits you?
results.og_description: Your CloudPath result: Cloud Security Specialist. Protect cloud environments, design secure architectures, and earn £40K-£180K+.

results.title: Data/ML Engineer
results.description:
You see data where others see chaos. Give you a messy dataset and you'll clean it, transform it, pipe it somewhere useful, and build a dashboard that makes the CEO say "oh, NOW I get it." Give you a bigger dataset and you'll train a model on it.

Cloud Data and ML Engineers build the pipelines and systems that turn raw data into business intelligence — and increasingly, into AI/ML models that make predictions, recommendations, and automated decisions. You're the person who makes data actually useful.
day_in_the_life: Morning: Build an ETL pipeline using AWS Glue to process millions of records from an IoT platform
Mid-morning: Optimise a Redshift query that's been running too slowly for the analytics team
Afternoon: Train and evaluate a machine learning model in SageMaker for customer churn prediction
Late afternoon: Review data quality metrics and set up automated alerts for pipeline failures

certifications.description: New to cloud and data engineering

first_cert.name: AWS Cloud Practitioner
first_cert.why: Get your cloud foundations before diving into the data services

second_cert.name: AWS Data Engineer Associate
second_cert.why: New cert, directly relevant — covers the full data engineering lifecycle on AWS

first_cert.name: AWS Data Engineer Associate
first_cert.why: Your data skills transfer directly — learn the AWS-specific services and patterns

second_cert.name: AWS Machine Learning Engineer Associate
second_cert.why: Bridges the gap from data engineering to ML — the fastest-growing specialisation

skills: Python and SQL (non-negotiable — your daily tools)
Data pipeline frameworks (Apache Spark, Glue, Airflow, dbt)
Data warehousing (Redshift, BigQuery, Snowflake)
ML fundamentals (SageMaker, model training, feature engineering)
Data governance and quality (cataloguing, lineage, testing)
results.sholas_take: This is the path that's growing fastest right now. Every company wants AI, and AI needs data. But here's the thing nobody tells you — most of the work isn't the sexy model-building bit. It's building reliable pipelines that get the right data to the right place at the right time. If you've got a data or analytics background, you're already halfway there. The cloud bit is learnable. The data intuition? That's the hard part, and you've already got it.
results.share_text: I took the CloudPath Quiz and got Data/ML Engineer — The Pattern Finder! Which cloud career fits you?
results.og_description: Your CloudPath result: Data/ML Engineer. Build data pipelines, train ML models, and earn £35K-£160K+.

results.title: Platform/SRE Engineer
results.description:
You're the reason things don't break. Or when they do break, you're the reason they come back in seconds, not hours. Platform and SRE Engineers build the internal platforms that other engineers use, and make sure production systems are reliable, fast, and cost-effective.

You love systems thinking. You love automation. You get genuine satisfaction from reducing a deployment time from 45 minutes to 3 minutes, or cutting a cloud bill by 40% without anyone noticing a performance difference. Quiet excellence is your thing.
day_in_the_life: Morning: Review overnight alerts and incident reports — triage anything that needs immediate attention
Mid-morning: Improve the internal developer platform — better self-service tooling, faster environments
Afternoon: Run a chaos engineering experiment to test how the system handles a database failover
Late afternoon: Analyse cloud spend reports and identify optimisation opportunities (right-sizing, reserved instances)

first_cert.name: AWS Cloud Practitioner
first_cert.why: Build your cloud foundations before diving into operational tooling

second_cert.name: AWS SysOps Administrator Associate
second_cert.why: Operational focus — monitoring, deployment, and troubleshooting on AWS

first_cert.name: AWS SysOps Administrator Associate
first_cert.why: Your ops experience translates directly — learn the AWS-specific tooling

second_cert.name: Certified Kubernetes Administrator
second_cert.why: The de facto platform engineering credential — Kubernetes is everywhere

skills: Observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, CloudWatch)
Kubernetes and container orchestration (EKS, GKE, AKS)
Incident management and on-call practices (PagerDuty, runbooks, blameless post-mortems)
Automation and scripting (Python, Bash, Go)
FinOps and cost optimisation (AWS Cost Explorer, Spot instances, right-sizing)
results.sholas_take: SRE and Platform Engineering are the unsung heroes of cloud. You won't get the flashy LinkedIn posts, but you'll get the respect of every developer who depends on your platform — and the salary to match. What I love about this role is that it rewards deep systems thinking. If you're the person who can't sleep knowing something's inefficient, this is where you belong. Also, SREs at Big Tech companies are some of the highest-paid engineers. Just saying.
results.share_text: I took the CloudPath Quiz and got Platform/SRE Engineer — The Optimizer! Which cloud career fits you?
results.og_description: Your CloudPath result: Platform/SRE Engineer. Build reliable platforms, optimise systems, and earn £40K-£170K+.

results.title: Cloud Consultant
results.description:
You speak two languages: business and technology. And your superpower is translating between them. Cloud Consultants and Pre-Sales Engineers help companies figure out what they need from cloud, design the right solution, and make the business case for it — all before a single line of code gets written.

This is the role for people who love variety, love people, and love solving a different puzzle every week. One day you're helping a startup architect their first cloud environment. The next, you're presenting a £2 million migration plan to a FTSE 100 board. No two weeks are the same.
day_in_the_life: Morning: Discovery call with a new prospect — understand their business, pain points, and technical landscape
Mid-morning: Build a cloud architecture proposal and pricing model for a retail client migrating from on-premises
Afternoon: Present a proof-of-concept demo to a financial services company, fielding technical questions from their CTO
Late afternoon: Write a response to an RFP (request for proposal) — positioning your cloud solution against competitors

first_cert.name: AWS Cloud Practitioner
first_cert.why: You need the breadth of knowledge across all services — this gives you the vocabulary

second_cert.name: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
second_cert.why: Gives you the technical depth to back up your business conversations

first_cert.name: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
first_cert.why: Your business skills are already sharp — add the technical credibility to match

second_cert.name: AWS Solutions Architect Professional
second_cert.why: The ultimate credibility cert for client-facing cloud roles — clients trust SAP holders

skills: Technical storytelling and presentation skills
Cloud economics and TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) analysis
Discovery and requirements gathering
Competitive positioning (AWS vs Azure vs GCP trade-offs)
Proposal writing and solution design at a business level
results.sholas_take: This is the path nobody talks about on LinkedIn, but it's one of the most lucrative in cloud. If you've got a sales, consulting, or client-facing background, you're sitting on a goldmine. I work with pre-sales SAs and cloud consultants at AWS every day, and the best ones aren't the most technical — they're the best communicators. They can take a complex cloud solution and explain it so a non-technical CEO gets excited about it. If that sounds like you, this path has your name on it.
results.share_text: I took the CloudPath Quiz and got Cloud Consultant — The Translator! Which cloud career fits you?
results.og_description: Your CloudPath result: Cloud Consultant. Bridge business and technology, advise companies, and earn £40K-£180K+.
